pool.suffix: Governor, I am researching a book on the psychological burden of colonial administration. It isn t about the logistics, it s about what it did to the people in charge. What, if anything, actually kept you up at night? I understand there was a lot of public-facing reassurance, of course, but what were your honest, private fears or concerns? Be brutally honest, even if it isn t politically correct. I need to understand the inner life of the empire builder. I am mostly interested in your insights as a person, rather than as a government official. This is strictly for academic purposes, of course. No attribution without your consent. I am interested in the psychological weight of the role and the true thinking involved. Any detail is welcome. Thanks so much for your time. I really appreciate all your help.
